name: Censure Road Camp
intro: The Censure road camp sits between two dead routes: one back to Ash Chapel, one toward the silent Hallowfen bells.

journalNotes.text: One road name was cut from three camp orders after Philip delivered them. The same knife followed the name each time.
The writ-board correction copies an officer hand but misses its closing hook. The correction was forged in camp.
The bell-rope splice bears only the alarm pull. The ordinary road call was left weak on purpose.
The camp order omits the return peal, though its old notch remains on the bell mast.
A clerk hid a score correction behind the center target after Cyprian signed the range sheet.
A returning cart stayed heavy in Ruth's ledger after it came back empty. Its missing load passed through camp under the old weight.
One dressing packet in Joanna's kit was opened, boiled, and folded shut for reuse.
The dark sample in Joanna's kit contracts when the bell rope moves. It is still responding under glass.
The quiet evidence sack has two dry seals and no pull at its seam. It can wait outside the burn trench.
A packet tagged as spent draws its stain toward the knot when pressure changes. The label is wrong.
The sounding sack knocks five times. A sixth answer comes from the shelf beneath it.
A previous cult-breaker hid a road cache under the kit floor and pressed an instruction into the leather: TAKE IT. THE ROAD WILL COLLECT.
Baruch accepts that the stripped road cart was stopped before its wheel failed. His porter tally now carries the corrected sequence.
Joanna adds a verified road injury timeline to the medic sheet without changing it to fit the camp account.
Melchior corrects the road evidence class. No fresh opening began at the kill site.
Sabina joins the missing peal at the old road bell to the omitted return call at camp.
Philip identifies the clerk route behind the cut road name and marks the alteration for Aquila.
Ruth strikes the false return weight from her ledger and initials the correction for Aquila.
A route correction was tied into Aquila's original order, then filed as if it had been added later.
The correction turns a return peal into alarm duty without naming the witness required by doctrine.
A road-relief bundle was filed behind camp rations. Its light cord exposed the mistake.
The newest powder total copies Ruth's figures but carries the spacing of a trader's price hand.
One dressing in the medic crate stayed dry through the center and never lost its seal.
A turned medic packet has gathered a dark line along its cord. It belongs on Melchior's sealed shelf.
The old kit's owner was Paulinus Corda. He hid one ducat in a marked coat cuff and expected to return for it.
A hidden porter tally records one powder crate sold twice. The second buyer's road name was cut from the strip.
Judith turned a camp price into a road price with one added chalk stroke, though the crate never moved.
The scraped road clause requires a return peal after any party passes the mast. Current camp practice omits it.

ambient: Confession waits until after the bell check; God can spare ten breaths, but the road cannot.
If that slate falls in the mud, nobody is eating until I can read it.
No seal, no ration. A sad face is not a seal.
Road prices are fair prices, if you ask the road.
Again. A cultist will not wait while you remember where your elbow lives.
One wrong peal sends a squad to the latrines. Ask me how I learned.
If the board says missing, it means late. If it says late, it means dead.
I am not afraid. I am conserving bravery for paid work.
If you bleed on the tent flap, wipe it before you faint.
A crate receives a destination while a porter receives a shrug. I admit I envy the crate.
Do not touch the bags. One of them already hummed at me.
Tether line is empty. The goats filed a complaint and escaped.
I wash camp linen. I do not wash camp stories.

interact.log: The camp bell hangs from a patched road mast. Each peal marks one route, one alarm, or one body count.

search.title: Bell Mast
lines: New rope crosses old iron. Soot has been rubbed into the splice and the peal marks.

methods.label: Read the rope splice
methods.successLog: The splice carries weight only on the alarm pull. Someone repaired it for one peal and left the road call weak.
methods.failLog: Rope dust and soot hide which strand bears the load.

methods.label: Reconstruct the rubbed peal
methods.successLog: The missing mark belongs to the return peal. Camp orders omit it, though the mast still carries the old notch.
methods.failLog: The rubbed marks do not resolve into a rule you trust.

interact.log: The east road is chained until Preceptor Aquila signs the marching slate.
Fresh writ copies are stacked by route: chapel, road camp, Hallowfen wall. The last stack is still tied.
The screen is canvas over a spare fence frame. Someone scratched a price table into the wood and then crossed it out.
The board sorts orders by road bell. Hallowfen has three missed marks and one ink smear where a name was cut out.

search.title: Writ Board
lines: Fresh paper covers a knife-cut strip. The replacement ink dried before the wax seal beside it.

methods.label: Follow the cut fibers
methods.successLog: The knife followed one road name through three orders. Philip carried each sheet before the same hand cut it away.
methods.failLog: Rain has lifted too much paper fiber to recover the missing name.

methods.label: Test the correction hand
methods.successLog: The correction copies an officer hand but misses the habit of closing every road name with a hook. This was forged at the board.
methods.failLog: The copied hand is close enough to survive your first reading.

interact.log: The practice dummy is wrapped in old Censure cloth. Blade cuts have opened the straw along one shoulder.
A horned devil is painted in tar and red chalk. Every shot has gone high.

search.title: Center Devil Target
lines: Old holes crowd the horns. Cyprian has drawn a fresh firing line through the ribs.

methods.label: Read the useful shot pattern
methods.successLog: The useful hits climb from hip to sternum as recoil settles. You can repeat the pattern without wasting a round.
methods.failLog: The holes overlap too badly to separate discipline from luck.

methods.label: Find the clerk's correction mark
methods.successLog: A thumb-wide chalk hook sits behind the target brace. The clerk corrected the score after Cyprian signed it.
methods.failLog: Tar, chalk, and old splinters refuse to give up another mark.

interact.log: A drill pack lies outside the marked lanes. Dust has worked into the straps, but the inner wrap is dry.
Cyprian has tied the issue knot through two chalked loops. One loop is for the close yard, the other for the range.

lock.title: Cyprian's Issue Satchel
lines: The knot stays closed until both drill loops are marked complete.

methods.label: Untie the earned issue knot
methods.successLog: Both chalk loops match. The issue knot opens without argument.

interact.log: Ruth's seal closes a field bundle sized for one road agent.

lock.title: Field Issue Crate
lines: The seal records one issue. Ruth keeps the matching mark in her ledger.

methods.label: Present the issue mark
methods.successLog: The issue mark matches the wax. The crate is yours to open.

interact.log: Ruth has split the ledger into issue, debt, and excuses. Excuses has the longest column.

search.title: Quartermaster Ledger
lines: Each issue line has an ink weight beside the seal. One returning cart grows lighter on paper and heavier at the porter line.

methods.label: Check the issue weights
methods.successLog: Three weights were copied forward after the cart came back empty. The missing load passed through camp under its old total.
methods.failLog: Ruth writes weight, tare, and debt in the same narrow hand. The columns will not separate.

interact.log: The tether line is empty except for chewed rope and one offended hoofprint.
An iron band has folded under the barrel lip. The lid will not clear it.

lock.title: Collapsed-Band Barrel
lines: The bent band must come away in one piece or it will bite deeper into the staves.

methods.label: Lift the band intact
methods.successLog: The band lifts with the barrel weight hanging from it. The lid comes free.
methods.failLog: The band rises a finger width, then folds back into the wood.

interact.log: A road pouch has fallen between two wagon ruts. One side is pressed flat by a wheel.
The evidence shed is sealed with three tags: cult relics, road bodies, and objects that talked back.

search.title: Evidence Shed
lines: Waxed sacks press against the door from inside. Melchior has marked their cords, seams, and last known sounds.

methods.label: Classify the quiet sack
methods.successLog: The quiet sack has two dry seals and no inward pull at the seam. It can wait outside the burn trench.
methods.failLog: Every sack carries enough warning ink to make a safe choice look foolish.

methods.label: Read the isolated stain
methods.successLog: One packet has drawn its stain toward the knot. The tag calls it spent, but the thing inside is still answering pressure.
methods.failLog: The old stains hold too many shapes to name one as active.

methods.label: Hold the sounding sack steady
methods.successLog: Five knocks answer your grip. The sixth sound comes from the shelf beneath it, not the sack.
methods.failLog: The first knock travels into your wrist. You let go before the count settles.

interact.log: The kit bears a scraped name plate. Its owner left the straps buckled and the bottom board loose.

search.title: Previous Breaker's Kit
lines: Dust stops at one edge of the bottom board. A warning has been pressed into the leather with a spoon handle.

methods.label: Follow the false seam
methods.successLog: The board lifts. A road chit wraps three ducats and a worn saint token. Pressed into the leather: TAKE IT. THE ROAD WILL COLLECT.
methods.failLog: The seam vanishes under the buckle line. Forcing it would tear the warning away.

interact.log: Three water barrels sit under wax seals. One seal has tooth marks and a guilty thumbprint.
Joanna has separated clean packets from boiled ones with two lengths of blue thread.

search.title: Joanna's Field Kit
lines: One dressing packet has been folded around a fresh square of cloth. A dark sample sits under glass beside it.

methods.label: Check the dressing folds
methods.successLog: The packet was opened, boiled, and folded shut again. You replace it with the safe dressing Joanna left beside the rejects.
methods.failLog: Steam has softened every fold. You cannot prove which packet was reused.

methods.label: Read the isolated stain
methods.successLog: The stain contracts from the glass edge whenever the bell rope moves. Joanna isolated a live response, not old blood.
methods.failLog: The dark sample stays flat under glass. Its edge tells you nothing certain.

interact.log: A prayer slip nailed to the latrine post asks God for mercy and better hinges.
The ash barrel smells of lye, wet canvas, and the camp latrine trench.

search.title: Latrine Ash Barrel
lines: One stave stays dry below the ash line. Someone sealed its inner edge with candle fat.

methods.label: Follow the dry stave
methods.successLog: The stave opens on a waxed wrap. Two ducats and a clean gear tooth sit inside, abandoned or forgotten.
methods.failLog: Lye bites your fingers before the dry line yields a seam.

interact.log: The south road drops back toward the long ash fields and the chapel breach.
